summary: Desktop auth must be explicit, bounded, server-validated, and unable to strand users on startup.
invariants: Startup auth has a hard 5 second user-visible deadline.
Auth initialization must remain correct under React StrictMode effect setup and cleanup replay.
Every desktop auth network operation has a bounded client-side and Tauri-side timeout.
Auth startup completion is idempotent; late work after the deadline must not overwrite the fallback login state.
Deep-link callbacks must never carry long-lived desktop session tokens.
Pending auth state must be generated locally and compared before code exchange.
Saved desktop session tokens are not trusted until the server validates them.
The app must clear local auth data when validation, exchange, refresh, or logout proves the local session is invalid.
Remote logout is best-effort after local session invalidation, so network failure cannot keep the device signed in.
Dashboard access is gated by server-returned user.planStatus and not by client-provided state.
The opener allowlist must stay limited to Diff Forge AI destinations used by desktop auth and pricing.
